result:
Build is green.

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/os_lib.rs` — `os_date` (line 455) and `os_time` (line 545) were Phase-A stubs that always used `t = 0` and a zero-initialised `TmFields`, so every `strftime` specifier (`%Y`, `%m`, `%d`, `%H`, `%M`, `%S`, `%w`, `%j`) emitted the empty string. The failing test (`reference/lua-c/testes/files.lua` line 800) builds a Lua chunk through `os.date(...)` and calls `load(...)()` on the result — with empty specifiers the chunk became syntactically invalid, `load` returned `nil`, and `(nil)()` raised "attempt to call a nil value".

**The fix:** Added three pure-Rust helpers to `os_lib.rs` — `unix_now()` (wraps `SystemTime`), `decompose_utc(t)` (Hinnant's `civil_from_days` Gregorian calendar algorithm), `compose_utc(tm)` (inverse), and `strftime_one(...)` (dispatch over standard specifiers). Wired `os_date` to call `decompose_utc(unix_now())`/`decompose_utc(check_time(...))` and to invoke `strftime_one`; wired `os_time` to call `unix_now()` and `compose_utc`. Operates in UTC (documented in the PORT NOTE) because `unsafe_code = forbid` precludes the libc FFI needed for a real `localtime_r`/`mktime`; round-trip remains exact.

**New state:** The "attempt to call a nil value" error after "testing date/time" is gone. The full file test now advances much further and fails on a different assertion (line 31, an `io.input` round-trip — a separate, pre-existing bug for the mega-loop to address).
